hook.text: If you've been told you need bowel surgery for endo watch this first
hook.emotional_mechanism_raw: Urgency and caution, prompting viewer to reconsider assumptions before making a decision about bowel surgery.
hook.target_audience_raw: Endometriosis patients who have been advised to undergo bowel surgery.

main_claim.text: Most bowel endometriosis cases are superficial and can be treated with shaving; full-thickness involvement is rare and requires a colorectal surgeon.

supporting_explanation.summary: The video explains that a recto-end or anal ultrasound scan (available in Cardiff) can determine how deeply endometriosis penetrates the bowel wall. Superficial or just-below-surface endometriosis can be shaved by a skilled laparoscopic surgeon. Full-thickness involvement through the muscularis propria (the 'barrier to poo') is rare and requires a colorectal surgeon to avoid complications.

funnel_rationale: Targets patients who have already been told they need bowel surgery, providing detailed information about bowel involvement levels and surgical planning, which is characteristic of the consideration stage.

secondary_raw: Surgical planning, shaving vs full-thickness resection, diagnostic imaging
